changes.motivation: Move the Go source code for the Terraform provider into the monorepo so all framework utilities live in one place, while keeping the external repo alive for Terraform Registry publishing requirements.
changes.summary: Created `packages/iac-provider/` by migrating all source from the external `terraform-provider-pf` repo: Go source files, `go.mod`/`go.sum`, docs, `.goreleaser.yml`, `terraform-registry-manifest.json`, `flake.nix`, and supporting tooling. The `.github/workflows/release.yml` from the external repo was intentionally excluded so releases continue to originate there.

changes.motivation: Automate one-way sync from the monorepo to the external provider repo whenever `packages/iac-provider/**` changes are merged to `main`, so the external repo stays current without manual intervention.
changes.summary: Created `infrastructure/demo_cicd/iac_provider_sync.tf` (Argo WorkflowTemplate using the existing `wf_spec` module with a `git_ref` parameter) and `infrastructure/demo_cicd/iac_provider_sync/sync.sh` (idempotent sync script: clone external repo → rsync from monorepo excluding `.git/` and `.envrc` → check `git status --porcelain` for a no-op exit → commit → `git push --force-with-lease origin HEAD:refs/heads/main`).

changes.motivation: Wire the existing Argo sensor in `demo_cicd` to detect pushes to `main` that touch `packages/iac-provider/**` and submit the new sync workflow automatically.
changes.summary: Modified `infrastructure/demo_cicd/sensor.tf` to add a `push-to-main-iac-provider` dependency using the existing `is_modified.lua` filter with the path pattern `packages/iac-provider/.*`, and a trigger that submits the `iac-provider-sync` WorkflowTemplate with `body.after` as the `git_ref` parameter.

changes.motivation: The sync script needs a GitHub PAT with write access to `terraform-provider-pf` to push synced commits; this credential must be injected securely from the existing SOPS secrets store.
changes.summary: Added `provider_repo_github_token` as a sensitive variable to `infrastructure/demo_cicd/vars.tf` and wired it from SOPS secrets in `environments/production/us-east-2/demo_cicd/terragrunt.hcl`.

changes.motivation: Migrated provider docs contain `<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->` auto-generated comments that caused cspell failures after migration.
changes.summary: Added `tfplugindocs` to the words list in `cspell.config.yaml`.

problems: The initial sync script spec used `git push origin main` which would fail silently or push the wrong commit because `pf wf git-checkout` checks out a specific SHA in detached HEAD state. The correct incantation (`git push --force-with-lease origin HEAD:refs/heads/main`) had to be discovered by examining how the existing `wf_tf_deploy` workflow uses the same git-checkout command. This is undocumented behavior of `pf wf git-checkout` that should be noted in its help text or README.

The initial spec used `git diff --cached --quiet` to detect a no-op sync, but this only checks the staging index and would miss cases where files are present in the working tree but not yet staged. The correct check — `git status --porcelain` being empty after `git add -A` — had to be substituted. The difference between these two idioms is a common source of subtle bugs and is worth documenting in the sync script with an explanatory comment.

The `tfplugindocs` spellcheck failure was discovered only after the provider code was migrated, requiring a separate fix pass. Because the docs directory contains auto-generated files with a predictable toolname comment, the cspell word addition should have been included in the migration task spec rather than caught reactively.
